I can't say enough about these guys.
God bless them in every way.
Thankful for the help you have given us so far and grateful you're there for people who need help.

I just want to extend a huge thank you to Jim, and his team here. Saying he went above and beyond for the safety of me, and my children, is an understatement. It was a huge amount of work, and figuring out what to do for my overly complicated case, and he was there every step of the way for our high risk case. These people work here because they genuinely care... I wish I knew of a better place to leave a good review, but 5 stars is not enough for the help we got, thank you Jim, and everyone at the Clark County Volunteer Lawyers program.
Thanks for your service
Outstanding legal advice and assistance for many areas of the law including family law, protective documents, tenant eviction assistance and more
Greta place Great all around
Great people I was so happy to have caring professionals help me navigate my court papers
Never got a call back

You get what you pay for, which is nothing. They do admit to not having enough lawyers and resources just to being limited and understaffed, yet super busy. It took a while for someone to call me back, but the woman who finally helped me was very sweet and took her time while on the phone with me. They weren't able to handle what I had asked them about, but were happy to direct me to other resources that could answer my questions.
Pretty nice digs for a 'Non-Profit'.

3.5 weeks of schedule/cancel/reschedule game of chicken. First attempt was 2 weeks to get to talk to someone. They cancelled 2 days before with only ever one time option for next week even after requesting an alternative. The second time they cancelled 3 hours before. Both times required my rearranging my schedule to accommodate their only one time slot per week. They reschedule for 11:30 on Monday. 11:45 Monday I called them, which they responded they were very pleased to know that I called before they could call me. Apparently the lawyer was just getting to the phone calls. Which was now my first indication that I was not the only person scheduled at 11:30 that day. When the call came at 1:30 p.m. it was very apparent that the lawyer was not prepared for my call because in order to talk about the detailed terms of my contract that I had sent over prior to last week's meeting, she was having issues with her computer and unable to access them.  So great that a nonprofit could use all that money to just reschedule people and not really give assistance. Very very poor form for a nonprofit. Will never go to then again!
A genuine group of attorney’s and helpers who are actually making a difference in Clark County.
Passion for Justice aiding folks who may not ne able to afford a Lawyer on their own.
They have not called back yet

I did not have to wait on hold for hours or be told the call volume was excessive and try again.  The volunteer lawyer called right on timd for phone appt.  She was very helpful and did not make me feel dumb like other free programs.
To many excuses why they can't get back to you.
So I called them Seeking advice on how to fill out some ppr work.  and when the lady found out how much I made she laughed at me and dismissed me.   Was humiliated and embarrassed.
No help

It takes 2 weeks or more to meet with a lawyer. That's me waiting for a phone call to come on no specific day ......for a very important cause. Wondering if the courts and judges are willing to wait and hold the case over until I have council......very worried about that. I will post more when I actually use this service. Ok 4 weeks later....no they cannot hold anything over for lack of council. Everybody has a right to have counsel even if you can't afford it and there's just nothing here to help. I am very unhappy with the lack of help for people who don't have the thousands of dollars for good representation even in probate court. Nobody really wants to help and the judges just want me to have representation and it's just not there to access. It makes me wonder how many people lose cases without representation? It's very sad and I love Clark county Washington it's so beautiful and a great place to live. I love the small town but where are the things that people need when you just don't have the funds? Not a lot of resources volunteers or avenues.
Ok, finally it's December 2020 and I left a message at their main place, not the call in number open from 9 to noon. Left a message and a very nice lady called me two days later. Had me sign up for the Navigate Law Group virtual group they hold because legal aid aka Clear Program, works closely with them and they can get an idea of how they can help me. So some bad news, due to Covid conditions the Probate lawyers are not available to meet at the normal place and the Clear Program has not set up their Probate department to help people yet. So December 20th is the soonest someone could help me! I signed up in September! I'm on the verge of two lawsuits and one brought against me without a lawyer this really sucks. Anyways I signed up for the Navigate Law virtual Clinic and guess what? No one called me no one verified my appointment and I've all but given up hope to be represented before I loose everything in an Inheritance Theft. Which is aweful. Everybody deserves to be represented and I have nothing, the superior court Judges really will not hear me in court. without a lawyer! No win situation and I will keep you posted as this goes on.
I haven't followed up on the referrals I was given yet, but I appreciate the kind advice
Because I never got a reply back. I have some legal issues that I need a lawyer to talk to about and give some advice. I didn't even get an email. I still need the help but don't have lots of money.
Helped me when I needed it they where all kind and very understanding.
Responded to our call. Was able to guide us on a family law matter.
Great Customer service 👍

They just need more people.  You can run inside for parking money - they usually have some.  Just make sure you don't get a ticket while you're gone.  There is a side entrance (with stairs).  Creepy basement level with all sorts of unmarked closets & the ladies' room at the furthest end.  No coffee, but a water tank.  If they can't serve you, they refer you to CLEAR in Seattle then CCVLP follows up with you.  That actually went fine.  Please consider volunteering here for office/attorney.  They definitely need it.  They are spread thin; the attorneys don't prep much or read your emails - it's more like drop in.  Some of them aren't used to handling eviction topics, so they might say one thing then back track & change their mind, so take it all with a grain of salt until they have thoroughly read their legal references as they talk to you.
Zero return calls
I'm getting sued by vulture debt collectors over a relatively small debt. Brian did a great job helping me navigate the paperwork and so far I've avoided a default judgement. Thanks guys!
So friendly

Matthew helped me resolve a landlord-tenant dispute successfully. He was responsive, clear, and professional, and he explained the legal reasoning in a way that made sense. I appreciated the direct communication and practical approach. Would recommend and would use again.

Horrible experience with LegalShield’s only Oregon firm (as of January 2026): Kivel & Howard LLP. I recommend canceling your LegalShield membership if this is your assigned provider.

I was assigned to Kivel & Howard LLP through LegalShield and worked with attorney Matthew Maline. The experience was marked by persistent communication failures, lack of case familiarity, and inaccuracies that ultimately forced me to seek counsel outside of LegalShield.

- Multiple written requests submitted through LegalShield’s platform—including requests to schedule a call—went unanswered. I had limited cell service and relied on written communication, which was repeatedly ignored. As a result, several calls were missed.

- When contact finally occurred, it was clear the attorney had not reviewed the case materials I submitted. I had to explain the basic facts of my situation from the beginning and correct multiple fundamental misunderstandings.

- The legal guidance provided was unclear and inconsistent. I was told no recommendation could be made, and that outcomes “depend on what the judge eats for lunch.” After further prompting, the only alternative offered was to send a demand letter seeking double the amount.

- When I agreed to proceed with a demand letter, the initial draft contained major factual inaccuracies, cited incorrect reasons for the demand, and was not fully completed. I returned written corrections via email (their requested method) and through LegalShield, but received no follow up for a week.

- The only explanation provided for the delay was that the firm couldn't view the legal documentation provided due to "password protection," despite the document being accessible and downloadable through LegalShield with no password protection. I communicated this twice in writing.

- By the time the firm confirmed they could access the documentation, my deadline had passed, and I was required to retain outside counsel to move the matter forward.

- The firm did not re-engage until after I filed a complaint with LegalShield. The firm also never responded to the revisions needed in the demand letter. LegalShield acknowledged the issue and asked the firm to contact me, noting that Kivel & Howard LLP was the only available provider in Oregon at that time. Consistent outreach occurred only after that complaint. By then, my case had progressed elsewhere and I canceled my membership.

Based on my experience, I do not recommend working with Kivel & Howard LLP or attorney Matthew Maline due to repeated communication failures, lack of case review, and inaccuracies in legal documents despite written and verbal documentation.

I cannot say enough good things about Kivel & Howard! The whole team at all points were incredibly knowledgeable, kind, and efficient. They were so informative and went above and beyond the entire process.  I would recommend to any friends, family, or loved ones. The best service and they know exactly what they are doing!

I've been speaking with a lawyer here for about 2 years using legal shield. His experience is now 3 years, and has the same ability as an overworked public defense attorney in terms of comprehension of a situation more complicated than 12th grade English.

Throughout the process he has shown his lack of experience through detailing when he got his degree and his lack of proper note keeping skills having to be reminded important details each call he makes which creates the impression that I'm wasting my money when I could spend the same amount with a local firm for the same result.

If he wasn't holding onto documentation about the process I started I would cancel the membership much to my and the billing departments pleasure as the quote for drafting a writ/motion apparently costs 2-3,000$ quoted by his coworker after saying I'm on disability (who also misunderstood what I requested from what I could tell but was not worth talking to)

I was eventually referred to a legal clinic that does not practice the area of law I am dealing with, creating a further disservice. if you need to do a last will and testiment. This law office is better suited for that but I would make sure to verify the phrasing to make sure it does not say the opposite or something else entirely.
"Best Law Firm in Portland. Steve was great."

Earlier this week I had Kivell and Howard law firm prepare a will for me, and the process was smooth and stress-free.  They explained everything clearly, answered all my questions, and made sure I felt comfortable with the detail.  Professtional, knowledgeable, and easy to work with----I highly recommend them for estate planning needs.

Do not recommend.

Gave very wrong consultations on 2 separate issues, bodily injury and disability rights.

Asked for another rep, they still haven't tried to resolve anything.

Attempted through LegalShield to resolve conflict with attorneys and directly through the company. Numerous attempts through email and phone contact. Zero resolution and resulted in losing ability to file due to statute time running out. They drew out the process, impeded my ability to seek appropriate council, was denied proper channel for recourse.

They seem highly inept.

Outright stated they don't understand ADA law and Tenant law after accepting my methodically documented case.

My bodily injury case was ignored after providing all the information requested. Sustained over 10K in damages but they neglected my case and avoided contact without due cause.

The one star is well-earned.

I have been a loyal LegalShield member for years, paying my fees every month. Recently, my son was involved in a traffic accident and received a ticket. LegalShield told me my son was covered under my plan, so they took my case.

After hearing nothing for two days, I called the law office directly. They refused to cover my son’s case, even though LegalShield said he was covered. This was completely inconsistent with what I was told.

Very disappointing and misleading. I’m now canceling my membership because I feel this service is not what it claims to be.
I cannot and will not recommend utilizing their services. Their effort, billing and response is not sufficient and crosses over to unethical.
Excellent!  Highly recommended.  KUDOS to Sophia & Kristen for outstanding customer service.  Also KUDOS to attorney Howard for assisting us.  This law firm is highly recommended.  A+++
So far mike has been very professional and has guided me through tough times. Know your rights!
Legal shield  and Kieval & Howard work together!! If you ask for another attorney good luck won't happen!! The whole North West u only have them!!

10/10 experience. Attention to detail, straight forward, ready to tackle problems and address them in an ethical, legal, and multidisciplinary manner. I highly recommend. Scott Howard knows what he is doing. I will trust him with legal matters for our family. An excellent lawyer for family estate problems in Oregon.

Thank You to all the professionals at this firm who are so courteous and organized!

I should not even be giving one star.  We had Legal Shield via my work and thought it was a great service, until we actually needed the service.  My mother's trust was being tied up by my brother, we contacted Scott Howard and he agreed to take our case.  We paid our retainer fee and Scott managed to get a copy of the will and trust that we were unable to get.  That is about all he did, not worth the $2500 we paid.  He then told us he was planning on setting up a court date in Multnomah County (where his office is located) we are located in Malheur county on the east side of Oregon.  I believe Scott found out he had to go to court in Malheur County and immediately tried to pawn us off on to other attorneys.  One attorney he tried to refer us to would not return our calls.  The other attorney was absolutely amazing and spent two hours on the phone with us, but apologetically told us he could not take our case due to the matter having to be resolved in Malheur County and he said he was surprised Scott took the case.  Now we had our answer as to why Scott was trying to dump us after being our attorney and wasting our time for nearly a year.  We tried to reach out to Scott several times via email and phone, he ghosted us and would not respond back.  Then three months later, we get another bill for $3500.   REALLY?  For what?  Scott did nothing for us but write a letter to the attorney representing my brother and getting a copy of my mother's will and trust where it CLEARLY states all court proceedings must occur in Malheur County.  Don't waste your money.  Scott will talk the talk, but he won't walk the walk. We now have a real attorney who is just shaking his head over what was NOT done and the time wasted with Kivel and Howard.
Used Kivel & Howard LLP with Legal Shield and I would recommend "Greg Woods", he was able to help with resolve a issue I was having within a few weeks.

Unfortunately the attorneys there do not wish to actually help you but will refer you to a third party website where you can do the work yourself plus the fee for the third party website...so why pay LegalShield anything? Or request help from this place? It's no worth the money especially when you have tae do it yourself anyway.

I recently had a fantastic experience with Kivel & Howard. Their professionalism, dedication, and expertise were evident from our first consultation. They paid meticulous attention to detail, examining every aspect of my case and keeping me informed throughout the process.

What stood out was their approachability; they explained legal terminology clearly and patiently answered all my questions, which eased my concerns. Thanks to their strategic thinking and experience, I achieved a positive outcome for my case.

I highly recommend Kivel & Howard to anyone looking for a law firm that truly cares about its clients.
This law firm screams "budget-friendly" 😒 The staff here sound so miserable and rude, even Kelly and Greg at the front desk. #LawyersOfMisery #BudgetLawyers #UnfriendlyStaff

Webinar on Wills was very well done! Mr. Howard was very informative and every once in a while, witty. He's a very good instructor!

Thank you, Sir, for taking the time in your busy schedule, to instruct 48 members of LegalShield on August 26, 2024.

Sincerely,

Mary Goode
Attorneys at Kivel & Howard have been very helpful and thorough in my many requests related to major life transitions.

If I could choose negative stars, I would. This is a firm works under the guise of Legal Shield. It's their business model. While they will boast about their 25% discount, it will be caught on the back end - leaving clients with at times no discount and at likely with a larger fee than what they would have incurred with a non Legal Shield firm. Scott Howard, in particular is to be avoided. He is not only not reputable, he's dishonest - and will take full advantage of a family in turmoil and use it to his advantage. At a time when siblings have lost their last parent, Mr. Howard not only did not offer condolences in any form, he pounced on the sibling/PR that "didn't need the money" and didn't seem to have any understanding of his fee structure, or how a simple probate matter should have proceeded. It was a perfect fit for Mr. Howard that ended up causing costly errors and a final attorney fee bill that was beyond excessive. There are so many other options for decent, honest probate attorneys - stay away from this attorney. He will not only divide your family, in a blink of an eye he will take a large portion of your loved one's hard earned assets that took a lifetime to accumulate. The reviews for this firm and Mr. Howard were there, the PR simply didn't understand or appreciate how important a bad/good review is for an attorney's reputation. Read all of the reviews for this firm carefully, and look up the turnover rate for staff and staff comments that are also readily available with simple on-line searches. There are good, reputable law firms, this is not one.
I have been a member of Legal Shield on and off for over 20 years. I have always had my legal concerns answered by the attorneys. I recommend everyone should have an attorney in their back pocket!

This law firm is contracted by LegalShield, and I’ve been able to get many of my questions answered, letters, written, and a couple of phone calls made through my Legal Shield contract through this provide a law firm. Sometimes they’re not able to call back within 4 to 8 hours within the same day, but they do always return calls the next day. I also like how you can set up a phone appointment whenever there’s phone tag going on, so that both of you and the attorney are available at the same day and time.

We’ve only used them three times over 10+ years, but each time it was a crucial, urgent matter. They have been wonderful. Twice in the past week on unrelated issues. Good expertise and customer service. Also, their explanatory webinars are improving.

Did their Estate Planning Webinar. It was very informative and helpful. I have also used Kivel & Howard for other legal advice and they have always been responsive in helping me understand my rights and options.
Informative and clear.
Love this company!!! So helpful!  Thank you
Amazing been a member for 15 years !!! Helped me with a traffic issue for free and other attorney wanted 3k for my issue. Unbelievable

It’s been few long months I called them emailed them paper work they couldn’t even keep organized. They keep on telling us to do bankruptcy keep on telling same thing for few months people save money or  find somebody else

Kivel and Howard are amazing!!! Their team was super helpful and informative throughout our Legal Shield case process. Their attention to detail and knowledge helped to get our issues resolved quickly. Would definitely recommend their services!!!

I want a solution to the landlord's unethical behavior. They are not abiding the law regarding repairs, disabled parking, illegal eviction notices, and have ignored their failed city inspections and ORS. This lawyer has not helped at all. I want to write a good review, help me and change the attitude of bad practice landlords.
Excellent every time! Thank you.
Great people helping people
Kivel & Howard has always been helpful over the years. If you have questions they will be there for you with answers and no judgment! Always a pleasure working with them.
I highly recommend using Kivel and Howard. Best lawyers explained everything super well and now I feel like I have a better understanding.
We are very pleased to have a business relationship with Kivel & Howard. They have always been very responsive and professional. Great value in the piece of mind we get working with them.
Very good information. My  clients and friends always have questions regarding these points. I will point them in your direction.
Kivel & Howard was very knowledgeable about the process to set up a will. Looking forward to working with them to complete my will.
I have had a few incidents that have needed legal counsel and Kivel & Howard have been extremely professional and helpful. So glad to have this Legal Shield service.

I have lost track of how many years I have been using Kivel and Howard,  Their layers take their time explaining things and never make me feel rushed.  Their advice has never let me down,  Having them a phone call away makes me feel safe.

I had the pleasure of working with this team, and they truly stand out as a remarkable resource. My recent experience with their attorney was exceptionally positive. Not only was the attorney Greg highly knowledgeable, but they were also incredibly easy to talk to and exceptionally personable. This combination of expertise and approachability made the entire process seamless and enjoyable. I highly recommend their services and appreciate the value they bring to their clients.
Kivel & Howard did a great job giving a brief overview of Estate Planning. Enough so we can get the ball rolling and get this task completed.
Howard was very informative and moved thru complex legal topics on estate planning in a very relatable way.

What a great lawfirm! I attended an estate planning seminar that was fabulous and very informative. The will is included in the membership, which is fantastic.
Thanks for the free meeting/webinar. Great value and really good information. We’ve been putting this project off and feel we can finally move forward.
Scott shared an estate webinar and it was very thorough and even some humor. I appreciated the time he took to answer questions.
Awesome webinar. Thank you for caring for us to get us motivated to get our affairs in order

If you find yourself in need of a medical malpractice attorney, it is clearly a time of trauma, fear, anxiety, and concern.  What you need is an outstanding attorney immediately, and one with the proven experience and intelligence to comprehend the gravity, complexity, and nuances of your unique case. Chris and the Kuhlman Law team were exactly that for me.  After working with them for several years, I can say with all my heart and gratitude that your case will be handled with exceptional experience, intelligence, compassion, and efficiency.  Call Chris and give him the opportunity to demonstrate that expertise and compassion to you.

As a physician/attorney, Working with Attorney Chris K has been great experience. Chris has practiced his profession with the highest standards keeping his client's best interests in focus. I recommend him in medical malpractice cases and other legsl cases
Lawrence Schlachter DDS MD JD

I've known Chris as a lawyer for years, and the highest compliment one lawyer can give to another is a referral.  I will always refer high-stakes medical malpractice cases to him when things are really on the line for the client because he gets results, he has the track record to prove it, and he'll try a case in a heartbeat.
Chris is an excellent medical malpractice and birth injury lawyer. I give him my highest recommendation.

I (with my two daughters) had an excellent initial consultation with Thomas. He explained many terms of which I had merely a vague awareness.  He was also a great listener: understanding my concerns over taxes, transferring wealth to my adult children, etc., and then offering some options that could address those concerns.  Finally, he was aware of our emotions and concerns prompting the visit to begin with.  A freeing experience!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Tim, for your kind words. We're glad Thomas could explain complex terms clearly and listen to your concerns about taxes and transferring wealth to your daughters. It was a pleasure meeting you and your daughters, and we're happy the consultation felt freeing. Please reach out anytime to continue planning or if you have additional questions.

reviews.text: My family and I met with Thomas for a consultation. He was so informative, knowledgeable, and patient. His sincerity built trust right from the start and I am so grateful for him treating my family with such care and personalized input

I've known Thomas since college, and he's one of the sharpest, most driven people I've ever met. It's no surprise he's built NW Legacy Law into an award-winning firm over the past decade. If you're looking for an estate planning attorney in the Vancouver/Portland area, I'd point you his way without hesitation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Sam, thank you for your generous review and for knowing and supporting Thomas since college. We're honored by your trust and recommendation and proud of the work NW Legacy Law has done over the past decade. We remain committed to providing thoughtful, client-focused estate planning services to the Vancouver and Portland communities. We appreciate you pointing others our way.

reviews.text:
We worked with NW Legacy Law to create a comprehensive estate plan for our family, including a revocable trust, Last Will & Testament, medical and financial powers of attorney, and guardianship provisions for our children. The entire experience was excellent.

We worked with Jake, who was an absolute pleasure to work with. He provided clear, thoughtful explanations that made navigating the many choices and implications of estate planning easy to understand. We always felt listened to and never rushed, even as we worked through numerous questions and “what-if” scenarios.

Jake and his team communicated clearly and consistently throughout the entire process. The planning and organizational materials—from early design through final documents—were exceptionally well organized, clearly presented, and easy to follow.

We feel confident and at peace knowing our plan is in place and appreciate the option to keep everything current through their ongoing client care program. We’ve already recommended NW Legacy Law to friends and family and would happily do so again.

NW Legacy Law  provides excellent estate planning services. From the start, I felt heard and genuinely cared for as an individual. Thomas is a thoughtful listener and very empathetic. While end-of-life planning can be difficult, he made the conversation feel comfortable and supportive. I truly appreciated the care and attention I received.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Kelly, for your thoughtful review. We’re so glad Thomas and the team made estate planning comfortable and that you felt heard and cared for. We appreciate your trust and kind words about NW Legacy Law.

reviews.text: Thomas was very easy to talk to and made everything understandable.  He answered all of our questions and was never impatient, which I have experienced with lawyers in the past.  Great experience overall with both Thomas and the rest of staff.
Extremely professional service. Worked with me for a few years through an estate process. They were very patient while working with multiple members of my family. They offer a great legal service.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Jacob. We are honored that you found our probate services helpful.

reviews.text: We have a Trust with NWLL and could not be happier with their service.
Amazing caring service. Will use again and refer to anyone that needs their help. Fast easy and affordable.
Thank you so much for making the process of setting up our trust so easy.  Guiding us thru the trust process with various options that work best for our scenario.

The attorneys at NW Legacy Law truly stand out due to their extensive knowledge in estate planning. They took the time to explain every detail, ensuring I understood the various options available to me. Their personalized approach was evident; they listened carefully to my concerns and tailored a plan that specifically met my family's needs and goals.

We established a trust with NW Legacy Law when we moved to Washington state. It was easy to set up, it was helpful, and  the process was efficient. It seemed we had done our best to prepare for raising a family and we were done before we even started.
Then my only stroke of luck in this story. I googled attorneys. First one that came up was Mr. Jenks. I called and within five minutes of speaking to these people I knew there was some hope for us. I mean from the second they answer the phone you are talking to somebody who knows a heck of a lot about the law. No run around talking to low level employees. They are all top notch.

In the end Ernest got me every penny I was owed. I got my job back and I knew people weren’t going to harass me at work for being unfortunate. He stuck it to my worthless/lawless/scam artist car insurance company and forced them to pay his fees separately from my money when they mishandled my case. That’s why experience matters. Another attorney may not have noticed when they slipped.

Ernest does, and he goes after it like a raging bull.
He does the best he can to help in any way.

reviews.text: I started my conversation with Keith during the most traumatic time in our lives. In March of 2018 our older two boys dad passed away in a car accident which was head in into a tree around 100mph. Flash forward 5 months. Mid August of 2018 my husband was out driving around with his adopted mother. She was in her early 80's and they were out in the country looking at properties because she wanted to move closer to us. She began getting car sick looking at the map so she asked if she could drive and, of course, he said yes. He was looking down at the directions and the next thing he knew was there was an Oregon State Trooper waking him up. She accidentally went off the road and hit a tree head on. The airbags in the car didn't deploy and he broke the windshield with his head. Ambulances finally arrived and got him out and up the embankment and her out. She passed away in transit to the hospital and he was trauma rushed in. I didn't really know what I should do because there were many issues I was dealing with. The cars faulty airbags which were serviced for a recall already, her passing and all the implications of that along with my husband's accident injuries, mental health and we owned a business I couldn't run without him. I called and left a message for Keith to call me and he did on his lunch break between trials. He talked to me about everything, completely patient and understanding, for over an hour. We live 2 hours away from him and he was willing to just hear me out and guide me. I asked if he would represent us and he said yes. There were multiple avenues we needed to take and him and Melissa (right hand lady, amazing!!) made us feel like they really took their time and gave us the best representation we could've had. It was a long road, a stressful, tear jerking, painful road but, they saw us through and made our stress in so many areas much easier and bearable. We honestly would recommend anyone to have the representation we received from them and will always tell people what a tremendous blessing they were for us.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for the kind words. We feel very fortunate to be able to work with nice folks like you and your family.

reviews.text:
After being struck by a distracted driver, I had several lingering health issues, which my car insurance was determined not to cover shortly after the accident date.
I knew I still needed treatment for my injuries and that it wasn't fair that I should be saddled with the medical expenses. I sifted through peer reviews and met with Keith.
He immediately put my mind at ease and assured me that his office would handle everything for me. It took over two years for me to feel moderately normal again, throughout my rehabilitation, Keith's office was checking in with me, making sure I was getting the medical treatment I needed and handling correspondence with my providers to assure that I wasn't worried about who was going to pay my bills.
Keith's office would often remind me that they wanted to make sure I felt better before they closed my claim. Knowing that I had Keith handling my claim took an immense amount of pressure and stress off of me and I could focus on healing. When I was ready to close my claim, his office processed payments expeditiously and answered my questions immediately. Always extremely professional and pragmatic, Keith was honest with me on day one about my claim and I knew exactly what to expect. The final settlement I received was fair and reasonable for both parties. If you're looking for an ethical attorney who will maintain an open line of communication with you, I would recommend Keith Dozier.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Jessica. We hugely appreciate the compliment. We enjoyed working with you and appreciate all that you've been through.

reviews.text: Roy Law Group saved my life. Literally. I was depressed, overwhelmed, confused, and terrified. I wasn't sure how I was going to survive. No one wants to be in a position where their disability finally demands leaving the work force. The long-term disability insurance process is not for the faint of heart... there is a lot of confusing paperwork and I was overwhelmed just thinking about it. I called Roy Law Group out of desperation and they were there for me in ways I couldn't have imagined! They did everything for me. Everything! They ordered all the records from my multiple doctors, helped my doctors know what to do when forms were confusing, corresponded with the insurance company on my behalf (every step of the way), and they reassured me and allowed me to vent when things took a long time. They let me know from the outset what they could and couldn't do. They were honest with me that I had a difficult claim, and we might not be successful... but they used that as motivation, not as an excuse. My claim was approved without an appeal thanks to Roy Law Group. I cannot recommend them highly enough! Because of Roy Law Group, I received the money I was due, for a service I paid into, when I was at my absolute lowest and completely overwhelmed by the simplest request. And a special thanks to Malorie at Roy Law Group! Malorie, you are a total gem to work with! Thank you for everything!
reviews.ownerResponse: Laura - We were happy to help. Your claim had challenges but it all worked out!

reviews.text: Highly reccomend....I live in WA and Roy Law helped me with my disability appeal. I mainly worked with Holly throughout this process and can't say enough nice things about her. All worked very hard on my claim, and I don't believe I wouldn't have got it approved without them.

reviews.text: Mark is an excellent attorney, and has won 2 big cases for me. I highly recommend.

So far, so good. Just hired Mark in principle based on the reviews. I'm a prolific reviewer myself and rely upon the voices heard here. I received a citation for 13mph over posted in a commercial rig.

I have yet to sign and pay my retainer agreement but I'm looking forward to his representation. There are less expensive options out there but I'm a firm believer that cheaper is not always better. Sometimes cheaper is just a way for some advocates to justify a negative outcome in traffic matters. If the retainer agreement is loaded with weasel clauses then you might want to shop around. Duh!

As with any traffic ticket anywhere, guilt or innocence is in the hands of those that are in power and are therefore willing to grant mercy. My expectation is realistic.

Mark will be my advocate and as such is afforded my trust and respect regardless of the outcome. I can argue the merits of my case in traffic court but the risk is mostly greater than the reward. It's a tax y'all. Nothing more than that unless you crossed the line and FAFO'd.

Lastly, I like that Mark vigorously advocates for himself in this forum. His detail and passion are quite remarkable. Do I think he will do the same for me in court? Hell, yes!

My hope is for the best deal possible and that's it. No deal, no harm. May the odds be ever in my favor.

Updates will follow.

Update: 2/16/26

Mark has negotiated an amenable settlement with the district attorney. It is less than hoped for but better than expected. Although Mark's fees are on the higher end, his service is worth every penny. Unfortunately, a positive review does not equal a rebate but in all fairness, I'm obligated to provide my readers with an honest assessment sans any personal animus I feel toward the Oregon State Commercial Code Enforcement regs.

You Oregonians really dislike commercial drivers, don't you? Levying higher fines on some of the most highly trained, highly skilled, safest operators in Oregon seems a bit out of touch with reality. Nevertheless, it is what it is. Mark is your guy if you want results.

I tried to enter an agreement with the other guy before Mark. But his Service Agreement was three pages long, contained at least two dozen weasel clauses making everything your fault if he doesn't win your case, and he refused to answer any of my texts and emails requesting clarification. Intentionally vague and convoluted? Most certainly, yes. He did, however, wait until the very last day before my trial date to insist on payment. Felt like extortion to me even at half the price. Fortunately, I saw the red flags early-on and hired Mark way before I needed him. Mark is my first call from now on.

Hopefully, I will not need his services again. Godspeed, Gorski Law and thank you for the negotiated settlement.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Kevin for those kind words.  I am continuing the work to try to reach the best possible outcome we can, always tougher for you professional drivers becasue of the special laws that apply to you folks  Thanks again for taking the time to write this, stay safe out there!

reviews.text: Amazing attorney, my results were better than I ever expected. Thank you Mark!

I have encountered many attorneys over my 69 years on this planet.  I have worked for them, faced them as a victim, as a witness, as a juror, and other various scenarios, but never have I found one to rank as an absolute Hero for the people!

I know that is strong wording, but Michael Fuller and his staff are incredible!  They genuinely care and surpass above and beyond!  I was taken advantage of by a greedy landowner and was facing the loss of all finances my husband and I had left.  We were turned down by several lawyers who basically stated the we "were screwed".  This incredible Underdog lawyer not only took our case, but had the Landowner offering to settle within the first few months!  The settlement didn't make us rich, but provided the losses we had incurred.  This amazing office didn't charge us a dime!  I couldn't talk them into any compensation be it monies, volunteer work, or anything I could do to repay their time, efforts, and genuine concern while displaying the best morals and ethics I have ever seen in the field of law.

I will never forget them.  I will sing their praises to every single person who believes they are too old, poor, or inept when it comes to the law.  That they are totally screwed when they are victimized by the bullies in this world!  Wrong is wrong!  If this firm can help you, they damn sure will!  They will always be KEEPERS and role models for the good in this world!  I am eternally grateful.  Ms. Jay
There was a lack of follow-up or transparency, which did not meet the expectations he had set for himself.
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm sorry you had a bad experience. As I understand it, you messaged with my associate attorney who spoke with me and then let you know we are unable to assist at this time. She provided you the names and contact information for other local specialists. She did not charge for her time. If this is incorrect, please let me know. Thanks!

reviews.text: ...now that SwickARd is his landlord, will he still sue the… ;) wish we had more lawyers like Mike.

**Take special notice of the comment left by the "Underdog Attorney". - EDIT120925: first comments erased, at some point their reply was replaced with a more benign denial. Appears they do read and take heed.
As a senior disabled in extremely exigent circumstances, tried to reach out to "Underdog" via his website for an email communication; no response.
I see from the other 1-star reviews similar problems, but what interested me most were the snarky dismissive responses from this self-professed underdog to those who left anything other than a fawning review. Any sincere underdog, who was truly concerned about people, would not be such a reprehensible bully to those who cannot defend themselves.
My worthless opinion is that if an attorney cannot or just plain does not want to take someone's case or help them with their particular problems should just politely say so. It's not THAT difficult to be minimally humane.
For those of you who have got money or can bring in some shiny media attention, you might stand a chance of getting this attorney to represent you.
As with all attorneys, it's about fame, power and money. But for those desperately needing help, when you roll the dice with these sharks, you have to keep in mind there's a high probability you are the one that's going to get bit.
Good luck to all those too poor or disenfranchised, America is on a frightful trajectory. And for those of us at Ground Zero, we're the first to be churned no matter what is at stake; no matter how wronged you've been, there is no "justice" for us.
reviews.ownerResponse: 20% of our cases are pro bono and we help as many people as we can at no cost. We are sorry we were unable to help you in this instance.

reviews.text:
Michael Fuller was recommended to me by my bankruptcy attorney. After getting assistance from my bankruptcy attorney with setting up communication with this law office, I would experience extreme difficulty. I was told to email one person who then told me to email another. They would only communicate with me via email. And since I have a disability related to communication, this proved to be extremely difficult.

They seemed interested in my case, but after some email exchanges, they preceded to blame my bankruptcy lawyer saying he did not serve the creditor who violated the automatic stay correctly. But yet, even when my lawyer would try to work with this company, they would refuse to follow up with either one of us. However, I was instructed by their intern to continue to send more information each time that the company violated the automatic stay. This was multiple times a month and ongoing. But I guess that was too much.

I would go several months before getting any communication back in return. They out of the blue scheduled a phone call with me last week. I took the time out of my day to take their phone call. After 30 minutes past the appointment time, I called their office to ask what happened. They stated that they were “backed up” and they would “get back to me shortly.” They did not.

I rescheduled with Kim for the following week via text message. I would receive another phone call later that week asking if I could reschedule. I asked them to check the calendar to confirm my other appointment. It seems as though their office lacks coordination in communication with one another.

I asked how long would the phone call take. They told me 15 minutes. So, in other words, I was able to conclude after sending them information for months and months, they were no longer interested in my case. They expected me to take two hours out of my day to sit around after hearing nothing for over seven months just to be canceled over the phone with a quick 15 minute phone call.

So, if this is you, I recommend that you ask them to send you a rejection via email like I did. But next time, I won’t wait 7 months or more to ask for such information. They gave me no apology or anything when I told them that I didn’t appreciate waiting over seven months to get rejected. Their only response to my displeasure was to correct me saying that I have four years to file a case against the creditor who violated me after my bankruptcy. No empathy, nothing. So, because it’s four years instead of one to file a case, I’m supposed to feel better about being ghosted for months on end thinking they would work with me but instead, showing me that I wasted my time?

Even worse, I believe they also must have gave me a bad name to the attorney they referred me to. The attorney they referred me to called me last minute to collect payment for a consultation that they would normally do for free. No thank you.

And for my bankruptcy lawyer to say that I was the first one to have this done to me by Michael Fuller’s office in 18 years… I think not! Maybe to one of his clients, but with all the other 1* ratings, I’m sure I’m not alone.

I was very fortunate to have Michael represent me in a case that meant the world to me! His courtroom demeanor is remarkable to say the least. I cannot express how much respect I have for this man, Ms. Emily Templeton and the rest of his staff.  Their representation throughout my case was flawless! My case was against two lawyers, and they hired representation! All 3 looked like silly putty in his hands! The jury deliberated for less than an hour and came back with a verdict in my favor! Several of my family members and associates are also legal representatives so I’ve watched a lot of court cases play out but few have I ran across were as good as Michael! I’m just wondering why he just has a 4.8 and out of 5.0 star review?!

I have never in my life been more disrespected or insulted, especially after being asked to explain in detail a traumatic incident, as I was by Emily Templeton. Someone from the office took all of my information, some deeply traumatic, and then called back saying that Emily wanted a consultation and she's so nice and caring and PERSONABLE, what a joke. Cut to next day, she calls, late I may add. Again she makes me go through every single detail that I had already given and she seemingly would have taken a cursory glance at. After all that she proceeded to tell me that she doesn't find cases such as mine "interesting" and that they are "boring" and "a lot of paperwork". She said she had no desire to even read anything related to my case. I guess since my case wouldn't be likely to make the news, it makes it not worth her time. It's all well and good to turn away a case but have some class and decorum when you do.

Congratulations read of your LEGAL advice on CIVIL RIGHTS and racial profiling along with Discriminative intimidation experiences we African American citizens have church to learn from.

Its difficult living in different colors of people. We make a difference for all if we care.

I care for Oregon too. Thanks Oregon NAACP'S 2024/2025 MEMBERS. ME, kenn,

I like to give a special thanks  to Michael Fuller , Emily Templeton, and your team for such amazing work with such difficult people in my case. Underdogs we are!

I wanted to take time to thank your office for being willing to take on a case, even though we had trials and tribulations with communication with the landlord and their attorneys. They violated many rules and regulations by showing up in small claims their Attorney. Knowing he was not Supposed to show up and even so he was able to file papers to take me to civil court. The evidence I supplied, the photos , and all the information I gave you was so accurate. We made it to a VICTORY.
Thank you and I appreciate all you have done!
UNDERDOGS we STAND! Love it!
Thank you for assisting me, and thank you for believing in me and taken this situation that was so difficult and confusing with a bunch of lies and confusion to a victory of the truth with the evidence that was supported by me, and you supply it into the courts and gaining a VICTORY!

You want these guys on your side, that's for sure. Michael and his team were kind enough to give me not one, but two FREE consultations, and one of them was even after their working hours. I was given good advice and my anxieties went down so much thanks to the thorough information provided. Michael and his team work for the people and are firm, professional, and so wonderful to work with.

If you're being harassed by shady debt collectors, just call The Underdog lawyer. Their team really puts in good effort to truly help people and not just for the money. 100/10 stars if I could.

This review is as to his character, and not to his office"s skills in providing proficient legal services.
I rarely need to hire an attorney -  I am able to win my cases without an attorney (I was briefly a bar member in another state until 43 years ago tragic life events made me detour).
I contacted Michael Fuller, thinking that he might see the value of my case (which could be a high profile one), and could offer pro bono assistance. Our backrounds are similar (mine a little worse, actually).
Instead, when I sent him a message through his website, I was ghosted. No acknowedgment. So you might know what to expect.
Ask yourself if your case will offer him money or fame? Am I right in thinking that the proper thing for him to do was to reply - sorry, not available?
My litigation is progressing fine without any legal help (but painfully time consuming, doing my own research).
Check his rates compared to the average for Portland attorneys!
If you get ghosted, like I did, there are resources for those without funds. Do your due diligence.
I'd be surprised if he doesn't defend himself here by mailgning me. I'm willing to bet against that!
Told me theres no money in defending the poor.
reviews.ownerResponse:
20% of our cases are pro bono, meaning free of charge. Our pro bono tenant rights clinic is exclusively dedicated to helping defend the poor.

More likely, you were told that we cannot accept your particular case, either because we don't handle your specific issue, or because we simply don't have the resources to help every person who contacts us.

reviews.text:
I called Michael Fuller when I was in great distress from a landlord situation.  He immediately took my case upon hearing the details.  He assigned Emily Templeton to be my attorney.  I was instantly impressed and greatly surprised by her demeanor and spirit.  She never once made me feel that I was unimportant.  She always kept me posted on what was happening.  Several months later,  we finally went to jury trial. Watching her in action was awe inspiring.  She really showed her knowledge and commitment to her profession. Class act all the way.. The jury was unanimous in all 3 points to my favor.
It was AMAZING! What a champion!!
Thankyou Michael Fuller and THANK YOU EMILY TEMPLETON.   You're a blessing to people like me.
I can't recommend them highly enough.

The Underdog Law group helped me combat my issues with my landlord.
Took less than a year from start to finish and Michael & Emily have been amazing! 🥰🙏🏼

update, as far as I know nothing ever came of this.
Hello Michael, thank you for getting back tome. I am having trouble finding the message that you sent with your cell number to call you.

My five star review comes with mixed feelings at this point and will be updated after I find out if he decides to reach out to us or not?  It has been over a week now but I understand that he is busy so I am still waiting for a call from him.
I am trying to find help for an amazing family of 6 that
I believe is experiencing what looks to be some serious medical malpractice and desperately need someone to help THEM for once.  I've watched them struggle so much and they have managed to make it through together one way or another.
Their Mom is so strong and has been struggling to do everything by herself for well over a year now.  She never asks for help but this time is very different in that her husband is in the hospital due to an infection in an incision after a surgery at Providence Hospital. He stayed in the hospital for two weeks after surgery and his bandage was never changed.
When she realized that it was still the same bandage that had been on there since the surgery she asked the nurses why they had never changed it they told her it was because it was a "special bandage".
Her husband had to return to the hospital due to the infection and is going to be there for six weeks .
Even if Mr Fuller isn't the right one to help this family I don't think it would be too much to ask if he could refere them to someone who can help.
reviews.ownerResponse: You can call me most anytime today at 503-222-2000. Happy to chat about your options at no cost.

Emily is levelheaded and humble, but was a fierce advocate for me as her client.  I am extremely satisfied with the services that were provided by Emily, Michael Fuller, and their team. reviews.text: Was very helpful with my case even though he couldn't represent me

I had a tricky decision about a reamortization or possibly paying off my house with my mortgage company that was in the middle of a bankruptcy and takeover but things were not exactly black and white. When it came down to it he gave me the advice that kept my cash position safe and helped me weigh the risks. A few years later I sold the house myself at the right time and it proved to be the best decision I could have made but wouldn’t have known until then. Thanks again for helping me make the right move when things were not so transparent. You truly helped me and I can’t thank you enough. It’s rare to meet people who care enough to help someone who has no one else to ask those difficult questions when a lot is on the line, thanks again Michael!

From the moment I met Michael nearly 10 years ago, it was evident in our conversations and shared time together that his shining character and kindness is truly genuine. Not only did Michael demonstrate a willingness, but a sheer determination to devote his time, and share his knowledge and expertise in law to provide aid to those who need it most in the community. Michael never owed me anything - but he still took time to aid me as a young adult seeking financial advice — and yet again, years later, saved my family from dire circumstances when a Portland, OR property management firm attempted to unlawfully vacate us from our residence. With Michael’s guidance we were able to immediately halt their actions and more than double our settlement, while giving us ample time to move on our own terms. Michael not only provided clear direction and timely advice, but more importantly, gave us the knowledge of law and negotiation prowess to fight for our rights in the future.
Great lawyers, fast and amazing service! Would recommend them to everybody!!

reviews.text: Matt handled my daughter’s personal injury case resulting from a road rage incident.  She was a minor at the time and so I took the lead.  She became an adult as the case progressed but Matt still kept me in the loop (with daughter’s permission).  The adverse insurance company initially claimed they were not responsible.  The incident was recorded (driver, car and license plate) and Matt coordinated with law enforcement, courts and both insurance companies.  By the time it all settled, the adverse insurance company paid the limits of their policy and our insurance company paid a settlement under our underinsured motorist coverage.  Matt masterfully worked every possible angle to get the best settlement for my daughter.  He was the consummate professional.  Highly recommended.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you very much. It was a pleasure helping you out.

Their commitment to ensuring we understood the process and felt supported made all the difference. I wholeheartedly recommend 3 Rivers Law to anyone in need of probate assistance. They truly helped us honor my Dad’s legacy with dignity and respect.

So very grateful,
Lorinda (and family)

reviews.text: We completed an estate plan with Jeff at 3 Rivers in May, 2024. Later, we met with his staff to complete the transaction, and they walked us through each and every page of our document with an explanation of how it all worked. Again, we knew we were in good hands. Again, all of our particular questions were answered.  I highly recommend 3 Rivers to anyone who needs estate planning.

reviews.text: Great communication and service. It was comforting to have the professional, knowledgeable assistance of the Three Rivers Law staff to guide us through this important process. Very pleased with the work done by this firm.

reviews.text:
Scott K. and his team at Forum Law Group were extremely professional and were a blessing to work with during the most difficult time after Dan was injured and became paraplegic due to being struck by a car while crossing the street in Portland, OR.
We were able to focus on Dan's healing while they managed the legal issues and all details of dealing with the various insurance companies. They served us in a timely manner and the settlement total was all we could hope for.  Without any reservations, we highly recommend Scott and his team as legal advocates for personal injury cases.
Aleta and Dan B.
P.S. Scott was appropriately at the top of the list our family created when searching for representation!

Words cannot fully express our gratitude for the brilliant work the team at Forum Law did during a very difficult time. They were kind, compassionate, and knowledgeable. The support and guidance we received throughout the litigation process were greatly appreciated and will not be forgotten.

It was a technically challenging case, but Scott, John, and all the team were experienced and organized. They communicated exceptionally well and kept things on track, and they worked with integrity and respect towards all parties at all times.

Their kindness, knowledge, and hard work proved successful, and we are tremendously thankful to each one of them. I hope I never have the need for this type of litigation again - but if I do, I will call them without hesitation.

Scott, his paralegal John, and their entire team did a phenomenal job. He represented my mom during a wrongful death claim involving my dad. Their expertise and clear guidance were invaluable during a challenging time.

This was so new to us, we had no idea what we were going into,
but Scott was very patient and very compassionate. He kept us informed during every stage, answering any questions my mom had. He is an experienced and knowledgeable lawyer.

John treated us with such care and kindness. He always communicated clearly, responded quickly. He made sure we were always informed with any updates.

They are truly the best!

Their true dedication and ensuring the best possible outcome for our family’s case is something I will always be immensely grateful for.

Their services are greatly respected and 10/10 recommended.

I had a wonderful experience being represented by Scott and his team.
He offered exceptional communication throughout the process.
He made me feel confident in our decisions as a team.
I 10/10 recommend them.
Scott and the whole team were absolute professionals throughout our case and settlement. They were honest and caring beyond my expectations.

Our representatives at Forum were outstanding throughout the process. They provided thorough advice and educational guidance throughout our case. We always felt that we were well informed and had great responsiveness to all of our inquiries. Beyond that, they were simply kind and trustworthy partners in some of the most difficult circumstances. Truly great folks there, all across the team.

I was injured outside my south waterfront apartment. Throughout my ordeal Scott was thoughtful, patient, and thorough. He treated me with respect and he kept me posted about the process. He gave me a clear idea about expectations but he also asked for my opinion along the way. Communications with John were clear and thoughtful, also.
I have complete trust in Scott and John and I would recommend their firm 100%.

Stephen and Liz handled my case with the utmost care, respect, and integrity. I appreciated most that I never felt intimidated to ask questions, or to reach out with any concerns on my mind. They were always responsive, available and, most importantly, kind. For an incredibly difficult stage in my life, they provided the support and advocacy I needed the most.

Scott and his team handled a case involving a tragic accident with my daughter. Scott was extremely kind and thoughtful. He was supportive of our family through an impossibly difficult time and dealt with the issues with extreme patience (our case took years to clarify for a variety of reasons beyond anyone's control). He was  very knowledgable about the related law and able to collaborate with other attorneys to get the best possible result for my family. I would recommend Forum Law Group to other clients without reservation.

Scott and his team were excellent in supporting our family after my husband injured his back. A serious injury that puts the primary income earner in your family out of work for several months with an uncertain future is very scary. From immediately following the accident through navigating the medical system, insurance, to an eventual settlement Scott and his team were accessible, kind and extremely competent. We would highly recommend their firm!
Great experience with Scott. I recommend him.

Scott Kocher:
When my sister was the victim of a hit and run crime as she crossed the street in her Portland neighborhood, I knew I needed to find her a good lawyer. Scott was recommended to me and I couldn't have been happier with how he helped my sister. His kindness, strategic thinking and consistency in ethical behavior remained constant. I highly recommend him!

I retained Scott Kocher six months after I was injured in a collision of my bike with a motor vehicle.  We sought, and Scott obtained, a settlement in compensation for my injuries that had resulted in two weeks in the hospital and several months of rehab. Scott, though busy, was always attentive to my case. He described his estimate of the settlement process, the timeline and the potential outcomes.  He came to our home to meet my spouse and I. Scott and I visited the accident site.  Scot and Lisa, his assistant, were clear, direct, honest and timely in all of their communications with us. My case went to arbitration, which Scott described and prepared me for.  We have received a complete accounting for my case.  I am extremely pleased with Scott's handling of my case, and with Forum Law.  I recommend them without reservation

Hiring Stephen Voorhees to represent me in my car accident litigation was the best choice I could have made. He is professional, kind, knows his stuff inside and out - and he helped me through a terrible, four-year fight that ended happily, thanks to his tireless efforts. He and his paralegal, Liz, were a true joy to work with, and they made a difficult process so much easier to bear. Highly recommend!

I was involved in a bike/car accident that racked up a lot of medical bills from surgery and rehab.  Forum Law Group not only helped me negotiate a settlement, but also took care of all the billing headaches, and helped me understand how the whole process worked.  They took over as the point of contact for all medical and insurance inquiries, which let me focus on my recovery, and made the logistics a lot less stressful.  I truly hope I'm never in this situation again, but if I am, I will definitely be calling Forum Law Group..

My kids and I were involved in an accident that was traumatizing for all of us.  Sarah and her team at Forum were responsive and sensitive to our needs - they handled all the insurance paperwork and worked with us step by step to make sure we were compensated for our injuries.  We felt cared for and supported.  Highly recommended!
I can't imagine how we would've handled our case without Forum Law Group's expertise, ability to listen, strong follow-up, and availability. Many thanks!
I would highly recommend Forum Law Group!  They work diligently for their clients to get them the results they deserve!
Steve Voorhees is an excellent attorney.

Thank you Sarah!
I was involved in an accident and the insurance companies were asking me to sign things I didn't understand. It was a stressful and overwhelming time. I had a friend who knew Sarah so I gave her a call to ask for help. It was the best choice ever.
Sarah was unbelievable patient and empathetic. She explained things simply and laid out my options clearly every step of the way. She let me make all the decisions, but she did all the work (and who doesn't want that in a relationship?).
Sarah was also exceedingly effective in managing my case. She has that big, official, persuasive lawyer side that you want in your corner. She was a wonderful advocate and brought all sides to a fair resolution. Couldn't recommend more.

Everyone At the Forum Law Group was responsive, compassionate and professional in supporting us through a difficult process in resolving every detail  that bewildered us following our traumatic auto accident.   We have the highest admiration for each member of the Group and recommend them without reservation.

Scott and his team (primarily Lisa) did a fantastic job on our case, and I highly recommend him and Forum Law Group. When we initially met with Scott, he was friendly and professional, and gave us an honest assessment of our case. Even though he told us there was some uncertainty about collecting a settlement, we immediately felt welcomed and comforted by his sincerity and obvious legal savvy. We knew we had made the right choice for legal representation. Then, throughout the rest of the process, Scott and his team showed great professionalism and level-headedness, even when we were emotional and frustrated by things beyond his control. Scott kept us informed, updated, and calm. In addition, he also translated all the legal language into non-legal terms, greatly helping our understanding. Furthermore, Scott promptly provided detailed answers to our questions, regardless of how many we threw at him. With his diligence and great expertise, we eventually reached a successful outcome to our case. Every aspect of his representation was professional and genuine, and we feel very lucky to have secured his services. We highly recommend Scott and Forum Law Group.

I had the misfortune of being in a bike accident and was recommended to go here.

I met with and end up appointing them to represent me. For me they represented a great balance. They were knowledgeable about the case,, entirely professional and  kept me well informed throughout, and were pleasant and courteous all the way through. I particularly enjoyed working with Stephen and Elizabeth and would highly recommend them.

I almost felt like a family member with the Forum Law Group.  My experience with them was the result of a bicycle accident and involved government agencies, an insurance company, and an engineering firm.  First contact with Forum Law Group to final settlement took almost four years due to complexities of the case.  A gratifying element for me was that in addition to a financial result we shared a public spirited goal to effect safety changes at the accident site, and were successful on both counts.

My primary contacts were with Scott and Sarah regarding the substance of the case and Liz and Lisa for supporting elements.  All of them exhibited a genuine warmth and caring that was extremely comforting during the process.

The professionalism, diligence, depth of knowledge and experience, attention to detail, availability, positivity, patience is exceptional.  I was always aware the decisions to be made were mine but I was comforted in knowing Scott and Sarah made certain that I knew all of the pertinent facts, possible outcomes, and risks so that we could make the best decisions.

I wouldn't want to again go through an experience similar to this accident and subsequent process, but if I were to, I would absolutely, unequivocally engage the team at Forum Law Group.

Thank you Forum Law team for a successful and gratifying experience.

After a car accident and unresponsive insurance folks, I went to Forum Law and began working with Stephen Voohees. I was very impressed with his expertise and his ability to  listen well and follow through.
Lisa and Liz were also wonderful. Each time I called, I was greeted with warmth, and I felt that I was not a "case" but a client in a shared process.
I would recommend Stephen Voorhees, Lisa and Liz at Forum Law. I was not looking for a "barracua" experience and I found they were respectful, optimistic and very ethical. Excellent all the way!

Scott spend endless hours, days and nights for over 2 years on making sure I had nothing but the best at all times. He was always on top of anything and everything that was thrown his way. No matter the questions I had or what time of day I always had an answer quickly and was kept very informed at all times. Everyone was always extremely honest and loyal. I would recommend Scott to anyone and everyone looking for legal guidance.
Joshua is very professional and a pleasure to work with. He specializes in just wills and trusts.
They got the job done and were friendly and casual.
Joshua made everything so professional, but not complicated in helping with my Estate planning.  Totally recommend him.

Joshua Pops LLC
From the receptionist to the Attorney they both make you feel comfortable and work with you. They are the epitome of professionalism.

Unfortunately I had a very good friend pass away leaving me to be the executor of the will.  Joshua was the attorney who created the will and in turn helped me navigate the probate process.   Joshua was excellent to deal with.  He was kind, very knowledge, empathetic and extremely responsive.   I would highly recommend him.
Joshua has shown my and my parents empathy and support beyond my expectations. I recommend his services highly.

I would highly recommend Joshua!!! We had to recently move my mother into an adult family care home and quickly realized the shock of the cost and everything that comes with it. We were referred to Joshua for elder care advice and decided within minutes that he was the best to assist us with the process. He is very down to earth and quick/easy to communicate with (he responded to emails while he was on a 2 week vacation) but also direct and to the point, which is what we needed during this process. The last 4 months have been quite a whirlwind of emotions, documents, trusting the process and it all happening so quickly but we were confident in Joshua.

The most misogynistic and ableist attorney i’ve ever met. This attorney refused to work with my family due to my siblings being disabled. When we met, Josh was wearing a sports jersey with a gold chain i kid you not. When I started to speak he held a finger up to ask me to stop speaking and said he would first meet with my dad then myself to talk about my late mothers trust, then after my father came out of his office he once again held his finger up when i began to talk and said he actually won’t be speaking to me at all until he reviews the trust.  I am a 30 year old woman, i know misogyny when i see it.

After my mother’s death I’ve dealt with plenty of attorneys for estate, trust, tax & conservatorship for my disabled siblings - so my point is i’ve met with at least a dozen  lawyers in the past year. And probably 2 dozen more in the last 10 years caregiving for my siblings.
Never have i ever dealt with any that arrived in a sport jersey and was so blatantly misogynistic. I felt like I was in an SNL skit, my god.
Do not go to this joke of a human being.

We were delighted Joshua Pops was able to revise a once complicated Revokable Will & Trust to simplicity not wasting any time to be thorough. We so appreciate his focus and friendly disposition. We could not be more pleased!

Joshua Pops did a excellent job in helping me get my husband on medicaid, he is very personable and easy to talk to and answer any questions you might have in a timely manner at a reasonable rate.
Thank you

After a death from dementia, Mr. Pops literally took over, handling the complete multi state estate, and simply telling me when, where and what to do. I had a health crisis at the same time and he managed the details to completion, did all the interactions, letters etc. and made the estate closure as stress free as he could.  He has a solid knowledge of Washington elder law. He has since done trust work and separate POAs for my family just as efficiently quickly and competently. I cannot recommend his no nonsense easy to understand approach more highly.

Joshua Pops is an outstanding attorney! I live in CA and my mother is in a Dementia care facility in WA. Mom needed to apply for Medicaid to cover her expensive care and I had no idea how to do this for her.  While calling around Joshua's name was mentioned several times as being an expert in elderly issues and Medicaid. My Mom's matter was relatively small, yet Joshua took it seriously and worked diligently to get the result my mom needed.  He was easy to reach, answered my many questions, and even kept tabs on the application once he submitted it, going above and beyond to ensure a good result for Mom.    I highly recommend Joshua Pops.

Josh Pops is our trusted family estate planning lawyer. Both my husband and I are lawyers and we would not trust anyone else to craft the estate and trust planning documents we needed to ensure the preservation of our assets and care of our children. Josh is extremely knowledgeable, straightforward, intuitive and detail oriented and is highly respected and liked in this legal community.

Super help and support :-)
